brand_tag: Yacht Management • IYT Training Center • Tours • Delivery • Registration
hero_title:
Practical experience in yachting
Sea travel, responsibility, freedom
hero_support: Comprehensive service and expert support for yacht owners, IYT Training Center in Montenegro, Sail Skill Association, and direct exchange of experience.
hero_highlight_1_text: Clear communication, practical decisions and hands-on support without agency noise.
hero_highlight_2_text: Cruising areas, weather patterns, marinas, places to relax, service berths, docks and fuel stops.
hero_photo_title: A personal brand built on seamanship, route sense and direct trust.
service_1_text: Professional yacht support, condition control, service coordination and seasonal preparation.
service_2_text: Preparation of future skippers through Bareboat Skipper and Yachtmaster programs with Sail Skill Association.
service_3_text: Trusted hired skipper for private trips, deliveries and Adriatic cruising.
service_4_text: Private sailing tours in Montenegro, Croatia and Italy.
why_1_text: Direct communication and tailored solutions for each client.
why_3_text: Montenegro, Croatia, Italy, Greece and Turkey with local route knowledge and practical judgment.
why_4_text: Calm, transparent and professional at every stage.
training_text: Not a heroic sea image, but a training environment: calm practice, clear discipline and skills that stay with a person on the water.
training_bullet_1: Practice on board, not theory alone
training_bullet_2: Sailing yachts, motor yachts and catamarans
destinations_text: From short day sails to multi-day passages and delivery routes across the Adriatic and Eastern Mediterranean.
destination_1_text: Bay of Kotor, Tivat, Budva, Bar
destination_2_text: Island hopping and curated sailing routes
weather_title: Current conditions for your day on the water
weather_text: Live weather powered by Open-Meteo for Kotor, Montenegro.
contact_title: Choose the easiest way to reach out
contact_text: Call, message or send a request by email.
cv_note_text: Use the CV button or choose “CV Request” in the form. I will review the request and send the direct download link personally.
footer_text: Practical experience in yachting and sea travel
site_menu_language_note: English is the primary version. Other language versions will be enabled as translations are prepared.
language_hint_text: Language was selected automatically from your device settings. You can change it in the menu under Language versions.
form_success: Your request has been sent. I will get back to you shortly.
form_error_required: Please fill in the required fields.
form_error_email: Please enter a valid email address.
form_error_server: The form is not sending from the site right now. Open your email app and send the message directly.
hero_photo_title_script: A personal brand built on many years of maritime experience, knowledge and trust.
destination_4_text: Ionian routes, charter handovers and island passages
destination_5_text: Aegean coastline, marina support and private itineraries
journal_intro: Photos, observations, practical answers and short field notes from passages, anchorages, travels and life on the water.
journal_compose_title: Publish a note from the ship log
journal_compose_text: Add text, date, photos or video. New entries are stored in the browser and shown instantly in the feed.
journal_compose_note: Likes, comments and draft entries are currently stored locally in the browser. For production publishing, this page is ready to be connected to any backend or CMS.
journal_tool_1: Dated entries with a clean card layout
journal_tool_4: Automatic English translation through free APIs with fallback
journal_tool_5: Responsive feed for phone, tablet and desktop
journal_translation_note: English text is generated automatically with free translation tools and can be edited later.
service_6_text: Independent handover checks, delivery runs and owner support before departure or after arrival.
service_7_text: Montenegro yacht registration support: documents, survey, required maintenance and authority communication.
journal_calc_intro: A working navigator’s tool for route preparation, calculations, and recording the main passage data.
journal_navdesk_intro: Calculations for passage, fuel, timing, tides and navigation planning.
navdesk_intro: Tools for primary data handling during a passage and while planning it.
navdesk_passage_intro: A navigator’s tool for calculations and recording the main passage data.
navdesk_tides_intro: A tool for preliminary passage planning in areas with noticeable water-level changes and depth constraints.
navdesk_window_intro: Enter depth, draft and under keel clearance.
navdesk_tides_placeholder: Select a place and date to load tide events.
navdesk_tides_suggestion_hint: Select a place from the list.
navdesk_tides_disclaimer: Reference information and preliminary calculations only. Verify all data against official sources and actual conditions before navigation decisions.
navdesk_modal_text: Nav Desk provides reference information and preliminary calculations. Tide data and passage-window calculations must not be used as the sole basis for navigation decisions, the safety of navigation, or the prevention of harm to people, vessel or property. By continuing to use this tool, you accept these terms.
navdesk_route_intro: Calculation of true courses, distances, and intermediate coordinates between departure and arrival positions for sea and ocean passages.
navdesk_route_table_empty: Route data has not been calculated yet.
navdesk_route_points_empty: Route points have not been calculated yet.
navdesk_ukv_title: Working radio exchange and standard message formats
navdesk_ukv_intro: Help with preparing short VHF radio messages: marina calls, vessel traffic service (VTS) calls, coast station calls, and message formats for distress, urgency, and safety traffic.
navdesk_tides_manual_active: Manual mode is active. Automatic tide data is not used in this calculation.
navdesk_tides_manual_table: In manual mode, the tide events table is not used.
navdesk_intro_description: Simple functional techniques for solving navigation tasks and giving seafarers practical prompts for more comfortable sailing and communication.
navdesk_tides_time_note: Time is shown in the local time of the selected place.
navdesk_ukv_spelling_intro: Converts words, names, call signs, and numbers into letter-and-number spoken form for English radio communication.
navdesk_ukv_spelling_input_label: Word, name, call sign, or number
navdesk_ukv_spelling_status_idle: Enter a word or phrase in Latin letters.
navdesk_ukv_spelling_status_empty: Enter a word, call sign, or number first.
navdesk_ukv_comms_title: Vessel profile and working radio templates
navdesk_ukv_comms_intro: Prepare vessel data, review the basic radio exchange order, and build working call templates with the main details inserted.
navdesk_ukv_profile_intro: All fields are optional. If not filled in, default training values are used.
navdesk_ukv_profile_status_idle: Fill in the fields or use the default training vessel.
navdesk_ukv_order_intro: A working sequence for building routine radio messages.
navdesk_ukv_marina_call_intro: Initial marina call before entry and a request for further instructions.
navdesk_ukv_marina_berth_intro: Request for an available berth with short vessel details.
navdesk_ukv_marina_reservation_intro: Short confirmation of a reservation and berth request on arrival.
navdesk_ukv_marina_service_intro: Request for basic services after berthing or before entry.
navdesk_ukv_intention_ph: Entering traffic area and proceeding to pilot station
navdesk_ukv_vts_initial_intro: Initial call to vessel traffic service with short vessel identification.
navdesk_ukv_vts_position_intro: Current position, course and speed report.
navdesk_ukv_vts_entry_intro: Report of entering the traffic area and further intentions.
navdesk_ukv_vts_traffic_intro: Request for traffic information and passage recommendations.
navdesk_ukv_coast_general_intro: Basic call to a coast station with vessel identification and purpose of call.
navdesk_ukv_coast_portinfo_intro: Request for current entry, weather, or port condition information.
navdesk_ukv_coast_assist_intro: Request for local assistance without declaring distress or urgency traffic.
navdesk_ukv_coast_relay_intro: Request to connect to a required service or relay a message through the coast station.
navdesk_ukv_distress_assist_intro: Position, persons on board, and assistance required.
navdesk_ukv_urgency_medical_intro: Urgency template without immediate danger to the vessel.
navdesk_ukv_urgency_propulsion_intro: Urgency message for loss of propulsion, manoeuvrability, or another serious problem.
navdesk_ukv_safety_weather_intro: Message about a weather-related or local hazard to other vessels.
navdesk_ukv_distress_relay_intro: Relaying a distress message for another vessel.
navdesk_ukv_phrase_pick_hint: You can choose a ready-made phrase from the list or edit it manually.

service_page_cta_text: The request goes to one main form, with this service already selected.
service_page_1_meta_description: Operational yacht management in Montenegro and the Adriatic: supervision, contractor coordination, guest readiness and preliminary estimate.
service_page_1_intro: Calm operational support for your yacht when you are away, between seasons, before guest arrival or during technical work.
service_page_1_include_1: Regular condition checks and practical owner reports.
service_page_1_include_2: Coordination with marinas, technicians and service providers.
service_page_1_include_3: Seasonal preparation, launch planning and basic readiness control.
service_page_1_for: For private owners who keep a yacht in Montenegro or the Adriatic and want direct, calm communication with a person who understands boats in practice.
service_page_1_area: Montenegro, Croatia, Italy and nearby Adriatic marinas depending on the task and season.
service_page_1_result: The owner receives a clearer picture of the yacht’s state, fewer surprises before departure and one responsible contact for practical coordination.
service_page_2_meta_title: IYT Training Center in Montenegro — Sail Skill Association
service_page_2_meta_description: IYT Training Center in Montenegro: Bareboat Skipper and Yachtmaster preparation for future skippers through Sail Skill Association.
service_page_2_intro: Preparation of future skippers through Bareboat Skipper and Yachtmaster programs. We offer you a chance to master another degree of freedom: becoming the skipper of a yacht. Our mission is to give you the skill and ability to create unforgettable time on the water for yourself and the people close to you. Real rest begins when the skipper and crew act with confidence and coordination; that is how good memories and unforgettable yachting are made.
service_page_2_include_1: A structured IYT path through Sail Skill Association: Bareboat Skipper, Yachtmaster, VHF and the next level when the participant is ready.
service_page_2_include_2: Practice on real sailing yachts, motor yachts and catamarans, not only classroom theory.
service_page_2_include_3: Local Montenegro practice with the rhythm of marina life, weather, berthing and real crew communication.
service_page_2_theory_1: Marine navigation, route planning, charts, lights and signals.
service_page_2_theory_2: COLREGs, meteorology, VHF radio communication and yachting documents.
service_page_2_theory_3: Yacht structure, safety routines, first aid and the logic behind skipper decisions.
service_page_2_practice_1: Handling under engine and under sail, with attention to timing and crew roles.
service_page_2_practice_2: Mooring, anchoring, buoys, marina calls and approach planning.
service_page_2_practice_3: Man overboard manoeuvre, use of equipment and the habits that keep the boat calm.
service_page_2_for: For beginners, owners, future skippers and crew members who want to understand the boat as a system and learn to act together, not just collect a certificate.
service_page_2_area: The Montenegro base is Tivat and Porto Montenegro, with nearby Adriatic training waters selected by program, weather and participant level.
service_page_2_result: After completing the course, the participant receives the certificate corresponding to the level of the completed program. The purpose is not to turn a person into a 'student', but to help a future skipper act calmly and confidently on board: to understand the sea, the boat, the crew, the weather and their responsibility.
service_page_3_meta_description: Professional skipper service for private yacht trips, deliveries and Adriatic passages.
service_page_3_intro: A hired skipper for private trips, owner support, passages and situations where calm practical command matters.
service_page_3_include_1: Skippering for private day trips and longer passages.
service_page_3_include_2: Route planning, safety checks and onboard coordination.
service_page_3_include_3: Support for owners who want guidance during a passage.
service_page_3_for: For owners, families and guests who need a reliable skipper for a yacht outing, transfer or sea passage.
service_page_3_area: Montenegro, Croatia, Italy, Greece and Turkey depending on vessel, timing and route.
service_page_3_result: A calmer trip with professional decisions, clear communication and attention to safety and comfort.
service_page_4_meta_description: Private sea routes for a day, a week or longer in Montenegro, Croatia and Italy with a skipper and live passage planning.
service_page_4_intro: The main format is a week-long sea route: several days on the water, bay stops, islands, grottos and coastal towns without the tourist race. A day trip is possible too, but the logic stays the same: weather, boat, group rhythm and good rest matter more than a fixed schedule.
service_page_4_include_1: A route plan for a week or longer: start and finish ports, passages, anchorages and weather alternatives.
service_page_4_include_2: The skipper leads the route, helps choose places from the water and keeps the holiday rhythm calm.
service_page_4_include_3: Grottos, bays, islands, swimming, photos, nights at anchor, marinas and waterfront restaurants where they make sense for the route.
service_page_4_for: For families, couples, friends and small groups who want a private week-long route, or a shorter trip built with the same calm logic.
service_page_4_area: Route ideas are chosen by weather, season, boat, starting point and the desired rhythm of the week.
service_page_4_area_montenegro: The Bay of Kotor, Bigova Bay, Luštica, Sveti Stefan and calm coastal stops selected by weather.
service_page_4_area_croatia: Hvar and the Pakleni Islands, a Krka route through the Šibenik/Skradin area, bays, island stops and waterfront lunches.
service_page_4_area_italy: The Amalfi Coast, Capri and Lipari/the Aeolian Islands, with stops shaped by season, logistics and waterfront restaurants.
service_page_4_result: Not just an excursion, but a personal sea route: a clear week plan, beautiful stops at the right time and calm decisions on the water.
management_mobile_quick_title: Yacht control while the owner is away
skipper_mobile_quick_title: Skipper for a trip or passage
tours_mobile_quick_title: A week on the water, calmly planned
service_page_6_meta_description: Independent yacht handover, condition check, preparation notes and delivery passage support from agreed start point to agreed destination.
service_page_6_intro: Independent presence at handover, condition notes, preparation for departure and delivery passage when a yacht needs to be accepted, checked or moved.
service_page_6_include_1: Presence at handover, external condition notes and photo record of visible issues.
service_page_6_include_2: Review of departure readiness: documents, safety equipment, fuel, basic systems and open remarks.
service_page_6_include_3: Delivery planning, weather window, passage execution and arrival handover notes.
service_page_6_for: For owners, buyers, brokers and charter operators who need an independent person at handover or a skipper for relocation.
service_page_6_area: The start and finish points are agreed for each case. The work is not tied to one country: the question is vessel condition, documents, weather, crew and a realistic route.
service_page_6_result: A documented handover, clear open points and, when required, a delivery passage completed with regular owner communication.
service_page_7_meta_description: Montenegro yacht registration support: documents, survey coordination, required maintenance and communication with the relevant authorities.
service_page_7_intro: Montenegro yacht registration is a real procedure, not an online button. We help the owner prepare documents, organize survey and required maintenance, and move the file through the relevant authorities.
service_page_7_include_1: Review of the owner’s case, vessel status and registration route.
service_page_7_include_2: Document checklist, translations or certifications where required, and preparation of the file.
service_page_7_include_3: Coordination of survey, mandatory maintenance items and communication with the competent offices.
service_page_7_for: For yacht owners, buyers and managers who need a local person to keep the registration process understandable and moving.
service_page_7_area: The main focus is Montenegro registration. Other registers can be discussed separately when the vessel, ownership structure and route make sense.
service_page_7_result: A prepared document path, coordinated survey and maintenance steps, and clear communication until the file is submitted and followed through.
ym_intro_title: Not a price list. A management structure.
ym_intro_text: Yacht management depends on the yacht, the season, the crew, the marina and the level of readiness the owner expects. The goal is simple: keep the boat under control when the owner is away, before guests arrive and during technical periods.
ym_proof_1_text: Regular checks, photos, issue lists and owner-readable reports.
ym_proof_2_text: Marina, contractors, access, quotations, works and timing.
ym_proof_3_text: Preparation before owner use, guest arrival, season launch or yard periods.
ym_services_intro: The model is split into a base monthly layer, monthly add-ons and one-time actions. This keeps recurring work separate from event-based requests.
ym_card_boat_text: Visual supervision when the owner is away: mooring, fenders, power, bilges, covers, ventilation and photo reporting.
ym_card_technical_text: Engine room, generator, chargers, pumps, air conditioning, dehumidifiers and basic system status.
ym_card_cleaning_text: Exterior and interior cleaning coordination, contractor access and quality control after work is completed.
ym_card_guest_text: Opening, ventilation, cleaning, linen, water, comfort settings, tender check and final confirmation.
ym_card_season_text: Spring commissioning, winter preparation, haul-out, launch, antifouling and service planning.
ym_card_sailing_text: Rigging, furling systems, winches, stoppers, blocks, sails and deck hardware coordination for sailing yachts.
ym_card_owner_text: A calm operational layer between owner, marina, crew and contractors: quotations, invoices, progress control and practical decisions.
ym_layers_physical_text: Washing, opening, ventilating, checking lines and fenders, preparing cabins and basic onboard routines. If competent crew is onboard, part of this work may already be covered.
ym_layers_control_text: Planning, contractor control, reporting, budget supervision, marina communication, technical schedule and owner representation. Crew can support the boat, but this layer still needs structure.
ym_calc_intro: Select yacht type, length, crew participation and services. The calculator shows an approximate market range, not a binding quotation.
ym_calc_type_help: Different yachts create different management workloads.
ym_calc_length_help: Length affects surface area, equipment, systems and coordination.
ym_calc_crew_help: Crew may reduce physical onboard work, but management and reporting still remain.
ym_crew_note_none: No crew onboard: the estimate includes a wider share of physical checks, guest readiness and basic onboard routines.
ym_crew_note_skeleton: Skeleton crew onboard: part of the routine work may be reduced, while coordination and reporting remain active.
ym_crew_note_full_no_captain: Full crew without captain: onboard routines are partly covered, but owner representation and technical coordination still need structure.
ym_crew_note_full_captain: Full crew with captain: management focuses on oversight, budgets, reporting and coordination rather than daily onboard work.
ym_calc_monthly_help: The base is one monthly item: yacht control alongside, technical control and owner representation. Yacht type, length and crew setup shape its price; monthly extras can then be added.
ym_base_item_text: Yacht control alongside, technical control and owner representation as one monthly line.
ym_calc_onetime_help: Add occasional actions for seasonal or guest-related work.
ym_logic_title: A clear range first, details on demand
ym_logic_text: The visible card keeps only final monthly and one-time figures. Yacht type, length, crew and service choices are adjusted in one modal, so the page stays easy to read.
ym_config_intro: Change yacht type, length, crew participation and services. The final price card updates instantly.
ym_docs_intro: Prepare a public estimate or draft proforma documents from the current calculation.
ym_info_text: The estimate starts with a monthly base, then adjusts by yacht type, length and crew setup. Monthly add-ons and one-time services are shown separately so recurring work is not mixed with event-based tasks.
ym_disclaimer_modal_text: The yacht management calculator provides reference information and preliminary budgeting only. It is not a binding commercial offer. Final price, scope, liability and operating terms are agreed only after vessel review and signed agreement.
ym_disclaimer: This estimate is provided for informational and budgeting purposes only and does not constitute a binding commercial offer. Final pricing, scope of services, liabilities and operational conditions are defined exclusively in the signed agreement after vessel review and scope confirmation.
ym_faq_1_a: No. It is an approximate budgeting range. Commercial terms are agreed individually after reviewing the yacht and confirming the service scope.
ym_faq_2_q: Why does crew participation change the estimate?
ym_faq_2_a: Crew can cover part of the daily onboard work. The management company then focuses more on control, coordination and reporting.
ym_faq_3_q: Do sailing yachts require a separate scope?
ym_faq_3_a: Often yes. Rigging, sails, furling systems, winches and deck hardware may add a separate operational layer.
ym_faq_4_a: Yes. Operational works can include contractor selection, task assignment, progress control and practical communication with the owner.
ym_contact_text: Send the yacht details and selected scope. We will review the situation and prepare a suitable management structure.
ym_model_base_text: Price depends on yacht length, yacht type and crew participation.
ym_model_monthly_text: Monthly add-on blocks: operational works, finance, administration and sailing control for sailing yachts.
ym_model_onetime_text: Event-based tasks: guest arrival, season launch, winterization, emergency visit, yard period, safety review, tender or provisioning.
ym_model_total_text: The calculator separates base month, monthly add-ons, monthly total and one-time services.
ym_calc_monthly_addons_intro: Add only the recurring monthly services that should be included above the base.
ym_empty_monthly_addons: No monthly add-ons are active yet.
ym_empty_onetime: No one-time services are active yet.
ym_monthly_battery_desc: Providing contractors, assigning contractor tasks and controlling work progress.
ym_monthly_machinery_desc: Financial activity for the account and on behalf of the yacht owner.
ym_monthly_ventilation_desc: Data analysis and processing, reports based on technical and financial records.
ym_monthly_rig_desc: Sailing layer: rig, running rigging, furlers, winches, stoppers, blocks and visible wear.
ym_one_guest_desc: Prepare the yacht before owner or guest arrival: open, ventilate, check key areas and confirm readiness.
ym_one_deep_interior_desc: Detailed cleaning of saloon, cabins, heads, galley, fridge/freezer and accessible areas.
ym_one_detailing_desc: Coordination of detailed exterior wash, glass, stainless, teak, polishing and protection.
ym_one_launch_desc: Season wake-up: systems, batteries, machinery, safety, cleaning and defect list.
ym_one_winter_desc: Winter or long-stay preparation: close-down, ventilation, power, covers, lines and control plan.
ym_one_emergency_desc: Priority check after storm, heavy rain, marina alert or owner concern.
ym_one_yard_desc: Coordination of haul-out, launch, antifouling, yard works, photos and contractor invoices.
ym_one_safety_review_desc: Review of key safety items, service dates and missing equipment list.
ym_one_tender_toys_desc: Tender, batteries, fuel, lines, basic safety and selected water toys readiness.
ym_one_provisioning_desc: Coordination of provisioning, water, ice, laundry, transport, restaurants and practical arrival support.
ym_base_scope_watch_text: Lines, moorings and bad-weather control. Ventilation, battery charging and washing at least once per month.
ym_base_scope_technical_text: Starting onboard units and machinery, checking operating modes and battery condition.
ym_base_scope_owner_text: Communication with the marina and service companies on behalf of the owner.
delivery_calc_intro: A simple preliminary calculation for a delivery passage: fuel is shown separately from skipper work. The final route, crew and timing are confirmed before the offer.
delivery_calc_route_status_idle: Enter the place in English: city, port, marina or bay. If the route is not found, type NM manually.
delivery_calc_route_status_missing: Enter both start and finish points.
delivery_calc_route_status_error: Sea distance is not available now. Enter NM manually.
delivery_calc_crew_12_15: Crew: usually skipper only; second crew member by task, weather and route.
delivery_calc_crew_15_20: Crew: closer to 18 m, a deckhand / mate should be planned in advance.
delivery_calc_crew_20_25: Crew: deckhand / mate is recommended; around 23-24 m it usually becomes a delivery condition.
delivery_calc_crew_40plus: Crew: second crew member and further manning depend on papers, insurer, manager and route.
delivery_calc_summary_empty: Enter sea distance to see the estimate.
delivery_calc_hint: Calculation speed: 6 kn for sailing yachts and 12 kn for motor yachts. Fuel burn is preset by vessel length and can be adjusted manually.
delivery_terms_crew_text: The calculator covers skipper work only. For yachts around 18 m and above, it often makes sense to discuss a deckhand / mate. On larger vessels, a second person may be required by safe passage conditions, vessel papers, flag requirements, the management company or insurer. This may be an existing contracted crew member of the vessel or a separately engaged specialist; their work and expenses are agreed separately.
delivery_terms_extra_text: Marina stays, port charges, forced weather stops, crew standby days, crew travel to and from the vessel, and crew provisions — food and drinking water — are paid separately unless agreed otherwise.
delivery_terms_route_text: The geography is not fixed to one country. Start and finish are agreed for the task; before departure, the distance is checked against the navigation plan, weather window and vessel readiness.
